The typical usage pattern for an effect separates initialization tasks from rendering tasks, allowing the effect object to be used efficiently inside an animation loop.

# GLKNamedEffect

**Protocol**

A standard interface for objects that provide shader-based OpenGL rendering effects.

## Declaration

```swift
protocol GLKNamedEffect
```

## Overview

Objects that implement the [doc://com.apple.glkit/documentation/GLKit/GLKNamedEffect] protocol provide rendering support to shader-based apps. An effect is expected to provide access to one or more shaders. The typical usage pattern for an effect separates initialization tasks from rendering tasks, allowing the effect object to be used efficiently inside an animation loop.

At initialization time, your app first creates a compatible context and makes it current. Then it allocates and initializes a new effect object, configures its properties, and calls its [doc://com.apple.glkit/documentation/GLKit/GLKNamedEffect/prepareToDraw()] method. Preparing an effect causes a shader to be compiled and bound to the current context. When an effect requires vertex data to act as inputs to the shader, your app also creates one or more vertex array objects. For each attribute required by the shader, the vertex array object should enable the attribute and point to data stored in a vertex buffer object.

At runtime, your app calls the effect’s [doc://com.apple.glkit/documentation/GLKit/GLKNamedEffect/prepareToDraw()] method to bind the shader program as the current program. Then, it binds a vertex array object (if necessary) and submits one or more OpenGL drawing commands.

## Binding the Shader Program

- **prepareToDraw()**: Prepares an effect for OpenGL ES rendering.

## Shader-Based Rendering Effects

- **GLKBaseEffect**: A simple lighting and shading system for use in shader-based OpenGL rendering.
- **GLKReflectionMapEffect**: A lighting and shading system that supports reflection mapping for use in shader-based OpenGL rendering.
- **GLKSkyboxEffect**: A simple skybox visual effect for use in shader-based OpenGL rendering.

## Conforming Types

- GLKBaseEffect
- GLKReflectionMapEffect
- GLKSkyboxEffect
